intent: Add layout block showcase body to featured World Cup article

children.text: The 2026 World Cup field is taking shape faster than the betting markets can adjust. Below we break down the ten sides with the clearest path to lifting the trophy — and where the value still lives for bettors.

body.title: Teams with real championship equity
body.subtitle: Form, draw luck, and squad depth — ranked for bettors, not pundits.

body.quote: The market still underrates how much a favorable knockout path is worth in a 48-team format.

body.title: Top contenders at a glance
body.subtitle: Equal tiles — ideal for quick-scan team profiles.

items.title: Brazil
items.body: Deep attacking talent and a manageable group stage draw make them the co-favorite.

items.title: France
items.body: Elite midfield control and defensive structure travel well in knockout rounds.

items.title: Argentina
items.body: Defending champions with Messi-era continuity and tournament pedigree.

items.title: Colombia
items.body: Physical, direct style suits summer heat and travel-heavy schedules across North America.

items.title: Portugal
items.body: Short note: upside if young attackers click.

items.title: Netherlands
items.body: High ceiling when healthy — but injury history keeps the price honest for sharp bettors tracking squad news daily.

items.title: Outright winner
items.body: Brazil and France offer the cleanest paths. Argentina remains live at a slight discount to 2022.

children.text: Lines move fast once group draws finalize. Bookmark this page and check back as friendlies resume — we'll update prices and paths weekly through kickoff.
